title: Employers say they prefer rebate-free PBM models
summary: More than 90% of employers agreed a rebate-free approach is easier to understand and would improve drug price transparency, according to the survey conducted for Evernorth, which operates a pharmacy benefit manager.

title: EEOC sues construction company for allegedly allowing ‘anti-American’ slurs against workers
summary: An American employee was derided by his Mexican co-workers for not being able to speak Spanish fluently, the agency said.

title: Kroger facility’s parking lot policy violated federal labor law, NLRB judge finds
summary: The decision builds off past agency rulings regarding employees’ on-premises solicitation for union-organizing purposes.

title: Heading into the workforce, the youngest Gen Zers seem cautiously optimistic
summary: Generation Z’s confidence at work has long wavered amid numerous societal shifts, previous research has shown.

title: Culture is critical for AI project success, Microsoft finds
summary: Only 1 in 5 workers are operating within an artificial intelligence “sweet spot” of skills and proper infrastructure, according to a report.

title: Recruiters see job applications triple — to more than 300 per role
summary: Data from a recruiting platform shows a “surge” in applications during the last five years.

title: CFOs earn higher pay as pressures mount, Datarails finds
summary: Median CFO pay now slightly exceeds chief operating officer levels, but tenure is falling and turnover is rising, the study showed.

title: Most workers told Monster their job harms their mental health
summary: With power now back in employers’ hands, some organizations have shifted their tactics and culture to devastating effect, surveyed workers indicated.

title: DEI training didn’t lead to hostile work environment, 10th Circuit affirms
summary: An attorney for the plaintiff told HR Dive his team will consider filing a petition for review with the U.S. Supreme Court.

title: Leadership development isn’t just for leaders anymore
summary: ATD found that all employees can benefit from such training, and that culture improves when it's offered.
